title: Wu says she’s secured commitments for nearly 500 teen afterschool jobs, is seeking 1500 more
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu secured private partnerships for nearly 500 afterschool jobs for Boston teens, aiming to secure 1,500 more.
The 2027 budget plan cuts over $5 million for the city's youth employment program, shifting funding responsibility to private partners.
Organizations like YMCA, Boys & Girls Clubs, and Artists for Humanity pledged to hire a combined total of 475 Boston teens.

title: Red Sox veteran has interesting response about Boston’s Fenway struggles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox are 9-20 at Fenway Park this year, struggling with home game performance.
Isiah Kiner-Falefa cites distractions and atmosphere change as reasons for Boston's home difficulties.
Boston averages 3.2 runs at home but 4.7 runs on the road, showing better away performance.

title: Bruins might have some tough decisions to make about Pavel Zacha this summer
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha is due for a significant pay raise after the 2026-27 season following a career-best year with the Bruins.
Zacha's potential contract extension is complicated by his value as a trade asset and the Bruins' salary cap situation.
The Bruins may consider trading Zacha for draft capital or talent to support younger players in their roster overhaul.

title: Berkshire County man charged with murder after body found in greenhouse
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Cole Bushnell, 41, of Sheffield was charged with murder after Michael Moore's body was found in his greenhouse.
Moore, 40, of Winstead, Conn., was discovered with blunt force trauma and a puncture wound, as reported by prosecutors.
Bushnell was arrested wearing stained clothing after returning home in response to false fire reports, the DA's office stated.

title: Pete Alonso homers for Orioles as Baltimore tops Red Sox 4-2
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Pete Alonso hit a two-run homer in the third inning, leading the Orioles to a 4-2 victory over the Red Sox.
Shane Baz pitched seven innings, allowing four hits and six strikeouts, securing a win for the Orioles.
Jarren Duran hit a leadoff triple in the first, but the Red Sox failed to maintain momentum, falling to 9-20 at home.

title: Mass. sheriffs’ offices run finances ‘like the wild west,’ Inspector General says
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Inspector General's report reveals Mass. sheriffs' offices' financial mismanagement including underfunding, overspending, and lack of oversight.
Sheriffs operated over 120 private bank accounts, with $42 million paid out and $36 million held in FY25.
Report recommends standardizing practices; Mass. Sheriffs' Association to evaluate findings for improved accountability and operations.

title: Hampshire College warns final-year students may not be able to receive their degrees after all
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Hampshire College warns final-year students might not complete degrees due to financial issues impacting the planned teach-out.
The college announced in April it would close after fall 2026, needing funds to ensure students can finish their coursework.
Financial challenges include stagnant enrollment, failed land sale, and $21 million bond debt refinancing difficulties.

title: Mike Vrabel on Christian Gonzalez’s absence: ‘Contracts are part of professional sports’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Christian Gonzalez absent from Patriots' voluntary OTA sessions due to ongoing contract issues.
Patriots' Mike Vrabel addresses Gonzalez's absence, citing contract business as separate from personal sports matters.
Gonzalez's contract, with salary set to rise from $2.2M to $18.1M in 2027, may contribute to absence.

title: What TreVeyon Henderson said about his viral Jaden Ivey tweet
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: TreVeyon Henderson's tweet quoted a Bible verse in response to Jaden Ivey's anti-LGBTQ comments and subsequent waiving by the Bulls.
Henderson emphasized sharing his faith despite backlash, stating he views life through a biblical lens and seeks to spread love and truth.
Patriots coach Mike Vrabel expressed respect for Henderson's beliefs, highlighting the team's inclusive environment while prioritizing education.

title: Here are the dates for this year’s Open Newbury Street
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Open Newbury Street will begin on July 12, spanning 10 Sundays until October 11, 2026.
Vehicle access is restricted from Massachusetts Avenue to Berkley Street from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. on event days.
Mayor Michelle Wu cited FIFA World Cup and other events for the altered Open Newbury schedule in 2026.
